mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding aggregate income and consumption, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS change and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops under 52%.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5 percent indicates restrictive monetary policy that raises discount rates and borrowing costs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than 45% of triggered instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production alongside rising unemployment generates a joint signal of collapsing supply and demand, causing sharply negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days that neither variable predicts independently.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to add marginal explanatory power (F-test p > 0.10) or the joint signal does not produce negative 60-day returns in at least 55% of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A low federal funds rate when CPI inflation is below 3 percent indicates accommodative real monetary policy that compresses real discount rates and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of low-inflation instances, or the return spread between low and high inflation regimes is not statistically significant (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Growth in retail sales advances reflects robust consumer spending and top-line corporate revenue expansion, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 5-day forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals rising capacity utilization and operating leverage for cyclical equities, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO momentum fails to achieve a Rank IC above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 60-day return frequency falls below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals rapid labor market deterioration and falling household income expectations, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The trigger fails to produce negative 20-day returns in at least 55% of instances, or the signal precision is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of falling housing starts and a high federal funds rate captures restrictive monetary policy transmitting into the most interest-sensitive sector, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term does not significantly outperform a linear sum of individual effects (F-test p > 0.10) over any 3-year rolling window.
